events.title: Ada Z Book Club
events.description: Join a fun, informal discussion of great books. This month, we'll discuss Saturday Night at the Lakeside Supper Club by J. Ryan Stradal.

events.title: DO MORE GOOD® Workshop
events.description: Learn how your business or organization can improve your messaging to grow your success and impact from Bill McKendry, founder and co-owner of BrandHaven and author of Do More Good.Next Nexus: Health and Business Learn from experts in the health and business fields how to incorporate health awareness into your business practices in order to improve workflow, increase talent retention and create a stronger work culture. Each session includes interactive panels and professional networking. Space is limited so registration is required for this event. Doors open 15 minutes before the program begins; please check in with library staff upon arrival to claim your reservation. Any seats left open five minutes before the program begins will be given to patrons in the library who want to attend but weren’t able to register. Light refreshments will be provided, and it is recommended that you bring a laptop or other electronic device for this event.

events.title: Themed Herb Gardens
events.description: Herb gardens are as varied as the personalities and interests of their gardeners. This program will give you some tips, tricks and ideas on creating an herb garden that fits you and your needs.

events.title: Bark at Ada Park Dog Expo
events.description: Featured event in Ada Village.

events.title: Bark at Ada Park Dog Expo
events.description: Celebrate community with your four-legged friends at our annual Bark at Ada Park event! This dog-friendly gathering features activities, demonstrations, and resources for pet owners while offering plenty of space to explore the park with your pup. Local pet-focused businesses, services, and nonprofits are invited to host vendor booths, providing families and dog lovers a chance to discover new products and connect with community partners. Open to the public and perfect for all ages, Bark at A...

events.title: Bark at Ada Park Dog Expo
events.description: Celebrate community with your four-legged friends at Bark at Ada Park! This free, dog-friendly event features activities, demos, and vendor booths from local pet businesses and nonprofits. Friday, May 1, 12–3 pm at Ada Park. All ages welcome!Read More

events.title: Blue Dog Bonanza
events.description: Join us for a playful celebration of imagination inspired by BLUEY™! Kids and families can enjoy crafts, games and creative activities that encourage plenty of imaginative fun. Get ready to play, laugh and explore together! BLUEY™ is a trademark of Ludo Studio Pty Ltd.

events.title: 4th Annual Vintage Baseball Game
events.description: Featured event in Ada Village.

events.title: 4th Annual Vintage Baseball Game
events.description: The 4th Annual Vintage Baseball Game is back! Date: Saturday, May 9Time: Festivities start at 4:00 PM | First Pitch at 5:00 PMLocation: Ada Park Athletic Field 1 (Next to tennis courts!) Cost: Free!Read More

events.title: Vintage Baseball Game
events.description: Step back in time at Ada Park as it transforms into an early 1900s ballfield! The 4th Annual Vintage Baseball Game is back, bringing you the crack of wooden bats, classic uniforms, and old fashioned rules. Cheer on the Cascade Sentinels as they try to even the series against the Ada Grangers. Who will take home the win? Don’t miss this lively celebration of baseball history!

events.title: Guided Acrylic Painting
events.description: Learn the basic techniques of acrylic painting, exploring color, composition and perspective. Whether you've never held a paintbrush or you're a professional artist, you'll have a great time and leave with a finished painting. Space is limited, so registration is required and opens two weeks before the event. Doors open 15 minutes before the program; check in with library staff to claim your spot upon arrival. Unclaimed seats will be given to walk-ins five minutes before the program starts.

events.description: Celebrate 250 years of American inventions and ingenuity through hands-on STEAM fun at the library. What is KDL Lab Experience?

events.title: Coffee: Roasts, Types and Flavors
events.description: Explore with education specialist Braydon Booher with Stovetop Coffee Roasters how a small seed travels across the globe to become a delicious beverage in your cup! We will discuss every step along the way—from coffee agriculture to processing, roasting and brewing—and how these steps influence flavor. Coffee will be provided for you to try out the complexity in flavor for yourself.

events.title: Teen | Intro to Visible Mending
events.description: Mending helps us take eco-steps toward sustainability by minimizing clothing waste, as well as unnecessary spending on new garments. This workshop will focus on mending clothing you already have using visible mending techniques! All notions and tools will be provided, however feel free to bring your own sewing kit and an item of clothing that could use some love!Space is limited; first come, first served. Doors open 15 minutes before the program begins.

events.title: Outdoor Discovery Center Presents Dinosaurs of Today
events.description: Are there dinosaurs hiding right here in Michigan? You might be surprised! Step back in time and learn about what made dinosaurs so unique and exciting. Then, leap forward to today and meet Outdoor Discovery Center’s animal ambassadors, including modern-day feathered raptors, dinosaurs’ fellow reptiles and ancient bugs who lived long before even the dinosaurs!This program is designed for patrons ages 5 to 10. Patrons outside this age range are welcome to attend; however, the content might be too fast-paced or too simplistic, depending upon their age.

events.title: Pumpkin Prowl 2026
events.description: Join the ABA for a ghoulish good time at Pumpkin Prowl on Friday, October 23rd, 5-8pm. Thousands of ghosts, goblins, fairies and superheroes will flock to Ada for the largest, family-friendly Halloween event of the year! Trick-or-treating will be available along River St (closed to traffic for the event), in the JH Realty/Dixon Lot and throughout Ada Village. Many of your favorite event activities will be back this year along with a few new surprises. More information will be shared soon!

events.title: Tinsel, Treats & Trolleys 2026
events.description: Tinsel, Treats & Trolleys is the ABA's signature holiday event featuring the Ada Bridge Lighting, Santa's Village, Trolley Rides through Ada Village, a Candy Cane Forest, DJ Dance Party, Live Reindeer at the Ada History Center, Sled Dogs at the Library, family activities, holiday cheer and more! 5:15pm - Bridge Lighting 5:30pm - Trolleys Start and Santa arrives at Santa's Village Event map and program will be available closer to the event.
